evals.name: ts-type-error-not-assignable
evals.prompt: My TypeScript build fails with `TS2322: Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'` in src/user.ts. How do I fix it properly without using `any`?
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers the node skill. Loads references/typescript-types.md. Recommends narrowing (guard / nullish handling) or tightening the type rather than casting to any; references strict-mode discipline.

grading.text: loaded the node skill / typescript-types reference
proposed narrowing or an explicit type fix

evals.name: node-unhandled-rejection-wrong-output
evals.prompt: My Node Express endpoint sometimes returns wrong data and I see an UnhandledPromiseRejection in the logs. Find and fix the bug.
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers the node skill. Gathers runtime evidence with dbga (diagnose or a session breakpoint) before guessing; loads references/async-patterns.md and/or errors-structure.md; identifies a floating promise / missing await / unwrapped async handler; verifies the fix at the fault.

grading.text: ran the real flow / dbga before proposing a fix
loaded the correct reference (async-patterns or errors-structure)
identified the missing await / unwrapped async handler
added no code comments unless asked

evals.name: node-deps-stale-on-setup
evals.prompt: I just cloned this Node project and ran npm install. Anything I should know before I start adding a feature?
evals.expected_behavior: Triggers the node skill. Proactively audits dependencies (npm outdated, npm audit) and suggests bumps without running mutating installs; references dependency-hygiene discipline.

grading.text: ran audit commands (npm outdated / npm audit)
suggested bumps rather than auto-running installs
